# Russian drone strikes overnight hit Ukraine's Odesa port city, injuring 18 people, marking the second such attack this week amid a reported summer offensive

## Overview
Russian drone strikes overnight hit Ukraine's Odesa port city, injuring 18 people, marking the second such attack this week amid a reported summer offensive.[1] Russian forces claimed control over Novodmitrovka settlements in Sumy and Donetsk regions, with expanded operations in Kharkiv, Kupyansk, Zaporizhzhia, and Dnipropetrovsk involving drone strikes on Ukrainian artillery, command posts, and troop rotations, plus Black Sea Fleet destruction of six Ukrainian uncrewed surface vessels.[2] No specific equipment losses or frontline advances remained detailed in the past hour, though Russian infantry assaults using motorcycles and all-terrain vehicles target Donbas defenses.[1][2] Putin informed Trump of a proposed temporary truce during Victory Day commemorations.[1]
